title: cinchona glandulifera
intro: Cinchona glandulifera is a flowering plant species in the family Rubiaceae, native to the Andean region of South America, and is closely related to Cinchona officinalis. The medicinal properties of Cinchona glandulifera lie in its bark, which contains alkaloids such as quinine, cinchonine, and cinchonidine, making it a natural remedy for treating malaria and other febrile conditions. The health benefits of Cinchona glandulifera include reducing fever, inflammation, and pain, as well as acting as an antimalarial and antipyretic agent. Cinchona glandulifera is traditionally prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and its extracts are also used in modern medicine to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and certain types of infections.
